item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I appreciate the detailed review! I am also glad you enjoyed it. I have taken most of what you suggested and edited the story accordingly. Thanks again!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm always excited to see an entry that has multiple 'layers'/entries like this; things like this (with multiple interviews and/or incidents), or big test logs like 093, or other things of that nature feel like the nice big ones I can really sink my teeth into and get lost in. In that regard, this absolutely did not disappoint.

I have some issues with the clinical tone/scientific language in various places, but I'm unsure of exactly how much that comes down to differing styles/tastes; I generally feel that entries should really go for the clinical language and specific scientific terminology as much as/wherever possible, as for me it's a huge part of what makes this specific format special. I'm going to include reams of specific critique in this regard; some, most, or all of which may not be applicable depending on your overall goals in writing this piece and your philosophy on that kind of approach.

+ Specific things I liked
- The Good
  • I was extremely happy to see 'sentient' and 'sapient' both applied here; that's an aggravating thing that I feel is very common (even here, where the distinction is correctly made in official guides/documentation/tagging policy)
  • I love the footnote regarding 'banquet requests', and a lot of my third collapsible is full of suggestions to get closer to this level of specificity in other parts of the entry.
  • I love when The Foundation treats something so clearly mythological/magical as something purely scientific; SCP-1512 does this well as well, and I love it every time it crops up. Rational analysis applied to nonrational entities.
  • The core idea of an SCP that is only contained because it wants to/has agreed to be there, not because it actually CAN be directly contained, is a great one that I thoroughly enjoy; I also appreciate that despite this distinction, it still received Keter rather than an esoteric containment class.
  • “This information can mean either SCP-5151 replicates this figure's appearance and nature or that SCP-5151 was this figure in history.” Excellent example of not folding implication into the writing; The Foundation looks at the data and charts possible conclusions because they can’t yet say with certainty. Love it.
  • The overall progression is great; the escalating story and reveals kept me interested the whole way through.
  • I really enjoy 5151’s discomfort at the end, especially because it’s ambiguous about if it’s because of the electricity or because it’s because he killed people who didn’t ‘deserve’ it by his metric; two angles by which The Foundation may have leverage over 5151, but they have no inkling of which and to what extent, still limiting their ability to contain.
+ Non-science/clinical crit
- The Bad
  • At the end of containment, I feel like ‘incapacitated’ isn’t quite the right word; perhaps “after SCP-5151 is rendered unconscious via [inebriation/alcohol consumption]”
  • “ the entity was found to be able to form” 'found to be capable of forming' would read better.
  • “When forming a blood-pact, SCP-5151 can establish an SCP-5151-2 instance with several subjects” There’s semantic confusion/conflation here by treating the mark and the agreement as the same thing. 5151-2 is previously defined as the blood-mark, and thus this should be ‘can establish SCP-5151-2 instances with several subjects simultaneously via a single agreement’, or 5151-2 should be defined as the semantic/conceptual notion of the binding agreement, with the mark being a physical manifestation of this (and perhaps defined as 5151-3, leading to something like “SCP-5151 can establish a single SCP-5151-2 instance with multiple subjects simultaneously, leading to [parallel/concurrent/whatever] SCP-5151-3 instances manifesting on all applicable parties”).
  • 5151 has solid dialogue, Dr. Moreau’s feels lacking in a way I can’t directly articulate. Just feels a little ‘off’ not outstanding enough to give him(?) noticeable voice or character, but not clinical enough to feel like deliberate detachment. (I wish I had better/more specific advice on how to punch-up the dialogue here).
  • “I will also need a retinue to train. Provide me with your seven best agents as you call them” needs some kind of punctuation after ‘agents’ to more clearly indicate how 5151 is saying this. “Provide me with seven of your best…agents, as you call them” or “Provide me with your seven best ‘agents’, as you called them” or something along those lines.
  • “however, several aspects concerning the nature of SCP-5151-2's nature has been discovered” have been discovered
  • Some of the repeat information/’as I have already said/as you already know’ in the third interview seems like it could be toned down.
  • Something about the structure of the final dialogue portion in Interview Log 5151.IL.04 from Pi-7-1, just doesn’t sound right. A little too much telling rather than showing (sorry I can’t be more specific in this regard).
  • “My theory is SCP-5151 was worshipped” I think “I theorize that” would read better.
  • I think the final memo where the true nature of 5151 is laid out is a little too direct about “this is what he is and his backstory”, but I’m not sure how to do it better, and something of this nature (a final summation plus some kind of bow on the nature & what happened) is definitely necessary; I’d like it to be more subtle and less direct if possible, but I’m not sure exactly how to achieve that, especially with a more obscure figure, and what currently stands is much better than there being nothing of this nature.
+ All the science & clinical talk
- The Ugly?
  • “No electronic devices are permitted inside of the cell except for an audio-recording device. All lights in the containment cell have been replaced by oil-fed lamps built into the walls.” Electronic and electric are not the same thing; based on the bit about the lamps and the later interview, it seems like 5151 doesn't like electric things as a whole (take, for example, an incandescent-bulb flashlight; electric, but not electronic, and The Foundation wouldn't want an agent bringing that in in the case of a power outage if possible). If you're worried that 'electric devices' might only imply things like tasers or portable generators, you could consider the phrase "No electrically powered or battery operated devices", which is redundant but thoroughly removes ambiguity, while being more accurate than 'electronic'. I'd also consider adding some wording about 'in non-emergency situations' or something else, as 5151 seems like he won't slaughter agents if they do happen to bring such a thing into his cell if there's some Very Important Reason, but they want agents to know to leave their cell phones at the door.
  • “Every Tuesday and Thursday morning, MTF Pi-7 ("Honor Bound Knights") is to enter the containment cell to train and spar with SCP-5151 for 7 hours.” 'Morning' is not defined thoroughly enough here for my liking. 0600 sharp? 0330 AM? Anytime between sunrise and high noon? Only between 0745-0953? Usually around 0830 but variable depending on 5151's mood, so ask him/it? Morning can mean a lot of things. It'd also be great to see a footnote attached to 'train and spar' that says something to the effect of "See Interview Log 5151.IL.04 for details regarding armaments"; when I was first reading through this, I thought that 'spar' needs to be more well-defined (Guns okay? Bare-handed only? Wooden weapons? etc), but that's broadly touched on in the later interview with one of the MTF agents stationed there so I think pointing in that direction is sufficient.
  • “Every Sunday evening, a wooden table with eight wooden chairs are to be placed in the center of the containment cell.” 'Evening' has the same lack of specificty as 'morning'. Also, do the kind of chairs or table matter? Only wood, or can a wooden chair held together with metal screws work fine? Does 5151's visual/style preference matter or come into play (IE furniture of a certain antique style), or will off-the-shelf modern wooden chairs from IKEA be fine?
  • “All members of MTF Pi-7 are required to seat themselves with SCP-5151 at the table” Does arrangement matter? 5151 at the head of the table, or anyone anywhere?
  • “chain mail armor from the medieval era” Country/time period/style specific would be nice. If it doesn’t resemble a specific historically-accurate type of armor, mention that; The Foundation would note something that matches ‘pop culture’ history rather than real history.
  • “although it references having many other names and titles in the past” Excellent place for a footnote that refers down to Interview Log 5151.IL.03 and/or Research Log 5151.RL.01
  • “subject's aorta with its longsword” The aorta goes through a whole lot of the body; does it sever it in a specific part every time, such as the neck, or does it sever it at whatever point, but always that specific artery? Either has different connotations. Also, there’s lots of different types of longswords; Foundation would either note that its sword matches a specific type/date/region, or that it doesn’t match a known classification (and would also note how that differs from its armor; armor and sword of very different styles/originating points in space-time is noteworthy).
  • “then proceed to drain the victim's blood from their body completely” Drain how? Restrain the target so they can’t apply pressure and allow them to bleed out? Hang them upside down like a pig? Suck it out like a vampire? Telekinesis?
  • “other adornments is made up of the same composition. This composition can pass through material objects and is impervious to physical damage. The entity has the capability to adjust this composition as it desires” ‘composition’ is used too many times here, making the flow awkward. What is it, exactly? There’s lots of ways to get scientific measurements without physically interacting with it (lasers, mass spectrometry, sonar, etc). Saying that these are all made of the ‘same composition’ rather than having the ‘same traits’ indicates that The Foundation can conduct some kind of material analysis, no matter how primitive or otherwise uninformative; whatever it is should be stated and then it can be noted that nothing else could be learned (“initial spectroscopic analysis indicated [blah], but SCP-5151’s anomalous properties have thus far prevented any further analysis of this nature”); if it is completely impossible to determine any amount of chemical/material analysis by any known method, that should be noted, and the SCP’s various bits shouldn’t be said to be of the ‘same composition’ because The Foundation would be unable to determine that; they would only state that they all exhibit the same trait of incorporeality & imperviousness.
  • Blood-pact footnote should have a clarification of meaning to delineate between social agreement and binding and magical/anomalous agreement and binding IE a blood-pact is something that normal people would do that carries social weight but is here being used anomalously.
  • “by creating a pact with their leader” This could use a footnote or parenthetical saying “exact limits of ‘leadership’ definition unclear, see Interview Log 5151.IL.01 for more detail”.
  • “Affected individuals have had a noticeable increase in feelings of inadequacy and dread” This should be something like ‘have reported a noticeable increase’; just saying they have it is ascribing too much for clinical analysis, the data The Foundation has is the self-reporting.
  • “as well as an increase in stress levels” This is something that can be scientifically measured to an extent; if at all possible, The Foundation would absolutely take vitals (heart rate, blood pressure) and do blood work (various things secreted by the adrenal gland, such as adrenaline/epinephrine and cortisol) and compare those against the self-reports and the expected levels corresponding to non-anomalous situations/medical data.
  • “Both men possessed large stab wounds through their aortas” A measurement would be good rather than just ‘large’ (something like ‘both men bore aprox 1.9 cm [lacerations/penetration wounds]’; number derived from blade width here as an example, use whatever measurement is right for what you’re going for]); also, mentioning the aorta but not body part implies that there isn’t an external wound, and the blade phased in and ONLY severed the aorta; if so, mention, otherwise mention the body part (IE “1.9 cm laceration across the neck, severing the aorta” or “victims did not possess any external wound, but autopsy/MRI/later analysis indicated that their aorta had been severed in their [neck/abdomen/wherever], despite lack of external wound, evidence of internal bleeding, or damage to surrounding tissue”).
  • “as well as their surroundings, were void of blood” Maybe something like “lacked any visible or forensic traces of blood”.
  • Given the above issues surrounding exactly what is happening vis-à-vis the murders, I’m unclear on why The Foundation would conclude the murders to be anomalous (lack of blood isn’t anomalous in and of itself, unless taken to one of the extremes above; it can mean a crime scene was cleaned) and thus unclear on why the third murderer would be taken into Foundation custody at that time. If any of the above more-noticeably-anomalous things apply here, then that clarification should clean this up; otherwise, maybe note it via footnote so the question is answered.
  • ‘Honoris’ should be mentioned in the SCP-5151-2 description; assuming that the pact between 5151 and Moreau/Moreau’s team is the only pact The Foundation knows of, then it should say “an anomalous mark onto a subject's hand (in all known instances, manifesting as the word Honoris, ‘honor’ in Latin, in glowing [color] script alongside a glyph or otherwise unknown meaning[assuming the mark is composed of a symbol as well or something more than just the word; if it’s just the word, then obviously ignore the glyph part]) when said subject forms a blood-pact”.
  • “historical figures from Roman times” A specific time period (a defined historical era, the reign of one or more rulers, a year-to-year range, specific centuries) would be great here; Rome lasted a long time and in several forms, and 'Roman times' can mean a whole lot.
  • In reference to Research Log 5151.RL.01, do any of the other figures have historical depictions/paintings/listed descriptions in historical texts? Would love to see a comparison of that listed (IE whether Czarny would have resembled Plantagenet or whether they would have looked nothing alike, either way it’d be something of note and interest to The Foundation).

Overall, an easy +1 (despite my various nits and picks and quibbles). This hits a number of notes of what I like about the SCPs as a format, and I hope to see it continue to be refined to a razor's edge.

item.title: Re: Upvoted simply because of 5151
item.content:encoded: Thank you! I certainly understand your criticism. I attempted to tackle an article that has several "it is hard to do that kind of article well" aspects to it. In my attempt, a byproduct displayed ineptitude on the Foundation's part as well as a long, sometimes drawn-out, narrative. However, it seems you caught onto and enjoyed the major goal I was trying to achieve, to feel an emotion when thinking about the entity. I intend to create a tale later on and will delve into more of his backstory, so stay tuned!

item.title: Upvoted simply because of 5151
item.content:encoded:
I dislike blackboxes and would usually downvote articles that have no justifiable reason1 to use them. I also dislike long articles because they tend to repeat quite a bit of information. You also fell victim to over-explaining the sequence of events that happened as SCP-5151 murdered MTF or researchers, an incident log instead of a whole 2 paragraphs for it is unecessary, imo. The Foundation also seems a little incompetent and stumped at how to handle 5151, but that's a plot point I can look over.

However, I really like the characterisation of 5151. I actually could sympathise with 5151 when he got sad that he killed his researcher friend due to his own blunder. It's like his morals conflict with what he actually wants to do: compromise his strictness for people that are close to him. Is that what happened to his brother as well? 5151 killed him, simply because his brother wasn't loyal but it might have been a mistake and 5151 has been depressed all these years.

You really put the human in humanoid. Love this SCP, you did a great job WhiteGuard!

Footnotes
1. Censoring names is not justifiable!

item.title: Re: Work of art !
item.content:encoded: Once again, thank you for translating it to the French branch!

item.title: Work of art !
item.content:encoded: I felt like I was reading a story in this report, a good story, of course!  I had a certain attachment to SCP-5151.  The relationship it has with the staff of the Foundation is unique to my taste, it's nice to see something really original.  That's just my opinion, of course.  It was a pleasure to translate it!

item.title: Re: Interesting Concept
item.content:encoded: Thanks for giving it a read! I am currently contemplating on writing a Tale about the SCP and it's brother, as well as a Tale about the MTF with the SCP.

item.title: Interesting Concept
item.content:encoded: Really loved the way the SCP interacts with Personell. It would be awesome if we could learn even more about his past and him being honor-bound. His backstory seems to be very interesting and I am yearning to learn more about him.

item.title: Re: Site Criticism Team - Mod Review
item.content:encoded:
Thank you for the criticism. I did attempt to tackle the issue with the Foundation and proceeded to trim down the article a bit. Thanks for giving it a read!
EDIT: I decided to give the article a rework as of 05/31 to better tackle some of your listed issues.

item.title: Site Criticism Team - Mod Review
item.content:encoded:
As I mentioned in IRC, this suffers from two main issues that kept me from upvoting it.

First, it's too damn long for the rather basic and predictable story that you're telling. I figured out that the blood pact was going to go sour literally as soon as the researchers made it. Your conprocs sell the reveal way too early, and then you put thousands of words between when a reader figures out your reveal and when it actually pays off.

The second, and much harder to fix, issue is that your version of the Foundation is being mind-numbingly dumb here. They cater to every desire of the SCP and give it all of the power when they have no reason to. They could have sealed the containment chamber immediately right at the start of the article and been done with it. He could have even been a Safe or Euclid anomaly if they just shoved him in a box and left him there. The incompetence of the Foundation drew me out of your plot before it had even gotten started and it helped further to telegraph your ending too early.

This is a -1 from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm a simple Pole, I see Zawisza Czarny, I upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thank you for the feedback! I believe I have addressed all of your concerns except for the hidden identity. As for the hidden identity, it was shown that the SCP repeatedly showed displeasure at revealing his Roman history. In the names and titles interview specifically, the SCP repeatedly told Dr. Moreau that it was unwilling to share what its original name was as well as its Latin link. Through this along with a couple of other spots in the article, I tried to establish the researchers' desire to find out the identity that the SCP was attempting to hide from them.

I hope that helps! Again, thanks for the feedback. I have taken your suggestions and implemented them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This was great when I read the draft, and it's even better now. Great 1st SCP. I would say that some of the doctors felt a bit stilted in the interview logs, but that could just be intentional for how they would talk to a scip. I have some very specific line-by-line stuff, but other than that, I like the story and progression.

Line-by-line
Sorry for being so picky

Consult the on-Site culinary team for further details on SCP-5151's "banquet" requests.

This line is fine, but the footnote is in the wrong place. It should be after the next sentence, when it talks about the banquets.

Despite the appearance of having clothing and armor, every aspect of SCP-5151's body, garments, armor, longsword, and other adornments are all made up of the same composition.

Technically, since aspect is singular and the list is a preposition referring to aspect, it should read "every aspect… is made up of the same composition" instead of "every aspect… are all made up of the same composition."

SCP-5151 has an anomalous ability to judge a person's character and decide whether or not they are honorable.

That's not an anomalous ability in itself. You should include something like "instantly" and/or "upon observing them," which is the actual anomalous ability. I can decide whether or not you are honorable (I'll say you are), but I'm not anomalous.

Current research is being conducted on the nature of SCP-5151 and the nature and removal of SCP-5151-2. Current research is being conducted on possible containment and restraint procedures for SCP-5151 by unconventional means, as well as the nature and removal of SCP-5151-2.

You basically just said the same thing twice. Unless a perfect containment method is found for an object, or if it's Safe class, then any research will be, at least partially, to find means of containment. Just take out one of them and it's fine.

MTF Pi-1 ceased their fire, and SCP-5151 proceeded to greet them with "Brave fellows,

Honestly, I'm not sure if this is fully incorrect, but it sounds awkward. Take out the "with" and put a comma after "them." (It should read, "proceeded to greet them, 'Brave fellows,'")

I would like to interview you, but would you first explain your reasoning for why you killed one of our agents despite sparing the remaining agents who were attacking you?

The word "but" implies a contrasting statement. For instance, if you said, "I wanted ice cream, but it was too cold," those two statements contrast. Those two statements in the article do not contrast, so I would say to just replace "but" with a period. (It should say, "interview you. Would you")

I swear I am the only one who hasn't lost their head over this new mark.

Put a comma after "swear."

In the future, harsher penalties will be administered if the Foundation ever witnesses a breach of security along the lines of an event of this scale.

I don't see any reason for "along the lines of an event." Just take that out so it says, "a breach of security of this scale."

Dr. Richter: But something still seems to bother you, doesn't it, 5151?

Why would he be able to tell that? Dr. Richter was just assigned to this scip. Maybe move the (visibly bothered) to before that line, or put something like that.

What do you have to say regarding this new information, 5151?

I see no reason for this line to exist. He asks if he has input after that, anyways, and so, to me, it just sounds really stilted. I say take it out.

I have concluded that SCP-5151's hidden identity belongs to Honos

"Hidden identity"? Unless I'm mistaken, nothing before in the article suggested that the researchers thought he had a "hidden identity." It was revealed that he had gone by different names, but it was very unclear. It's not a big deal, but I would say replace "hidden identity" with "true identity."

On a side-note, Site Director, my research team is currently terrified.

I know this is supposed to sound scary and threatening, and all that, but it really just comes off as funny to me. Just offhandedly telling your superior, "By the way, we're scared out of our minds," is just weirdly funny to me. I don't really know how you should change it, though.

Concerning SCP-500, that will be something that I will need to discuss with others.

This line feels kind of stilted. I think it's the "with others" tacked on at the end. I would suggest changing it to passive voice and just saying, "that will need to be discussed later in the future."

Either way, it's not a conclusive final line at all. Not saying that it needs to be, but especially for heavily narrative based articles, it just doesn't feel very climactic. Take the ending of SCP-5002, for example: "Reader, I very much hope that you feel the same way. About me, and about yourself." That hits hard. That's just a suggestion, though, not necessary at all.

Anyways, +1.

item.title: Well done!
item.content:encoded: I've helped work on this skip and I'm delighted with the results. This is a vast improvement from the article initially presented to me, and its execution has been greatly delivered. Very well done! +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks for the catches! They have been fixed. Also, I am glad to hear you enjoyed the final interview! I thought it brought things together well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hello, good sir! Congratulations on your first SCP! I was quite delighted to be able to give a critique to this beforehand, and your end result seems to be even better than the last time I checked it.

For real though, I really enjoyed the last interview with SCP-5151, since you gave some fragments of its personality early on, with that final bit showing that it might actually have some remorse for its actions. Good stuff.

To be honest, I was only a bit bothered by some minor SPAG mistakes, though I do have some improvements for that. Do keep in mind that this is coming from someone who really craves line-by-line perfection, so please forgive this manic man for his bad habit.

Line-by-line-
                by-line-by-line

All right, red and crossed out = deleted; blue = replacement.

MTF Epsilon-11 Commander's message

SCP-4878 has been returned to Site-119 and has been placed under enhanced security.

Addendum 5151.07: Incident Report 5151.IR.01

Pi-7-1 proceeded to fire a taser at SCP-5151. After being hit by the taser, SCP-5151 causing it to convulsed for a brief moment…[ ]

O5 Response

[ ]…Security Director Oliver Albury has been administered a Class C amnestic and has been demoted to D-class.

Gerard Hansjörg's message

Dr. Richter, Yyour input and concerns have been noted,…[ ]

Other than that, most of the stuff here is pretty good! Really glad to see that you managed to make an unkillable humanoid that's still interesting!

Also, you might need to announce your SCP in this forum so that it can gain some needed traction.

Good luck to you and a good day (or night) to all who are reading this!

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
Special thanks to Milo DeFrisco, Marcelles_Raynes, Recette, and Doorhandle for their suggestions and feedback!

Thanks for checking out the article! If you have any questions or suggestions, you can either comment below or send me a PM. I will try to address you in a timely manner. Have a wonderful day!
